ci_support_linux_64_cuda_compiler_versio:
c_stdlib:
- sysroot
c_stdlib_version:
- '2.17'
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cuda_compiler:
- cuda-nvcc
cuda_compiler_version:
- '12.9'
cxx_compiler:
- gxx
cxx_compiler_version:
- '14'
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
libblas:
- 3.9.* *netlib
liblapack:
- 3.9.* *netlib
pin_run_as_build:
  python:
    min_pin: x.x
    max_pin: x.x
python:
- 3.10.* *_cpython
spdlog:
- '1.16'
target_platform:
- linux-64
zip_keys:
- - cxx_compiler_version
  - c_stdlib_version
  - cuda_compiler_version

export CUTENSOR_ROOT="$PREFIX"

name: qiskit-aer

about.summary: Qiskit Aer - High performance simulators for Qiskit
Qiskit Aer - High performance simulators for Qiskit

raw_meta_yaml:
schema_version: 1

context:
  name: qiskit-aer
  version: "0.17.2"
  build: 4
  has_cuda: ${{ cuda_compiler_version != "None" }}

package:
  name: ${{ name|lower }}
  version: ${{ version }}

source:
  url: https://github.com/Qiskit/${{ name }}/archive/refs/tags/${{ version }}.tar.gz
  sha256: 0231c74ae23d7dcaef664c39e57f800ee5174f73821a60f328ca3fe542266950
  patches:
    # Update cmake configuration to base build flags on target architecture instead of host
    # Submitted upstream in https://github.com/Qiskit/qiskit-aer/pull/2390
    - 0001-Base-x86-compiler-flags-on-target-rather-than-host-s.patch
    # Replace lambdas in pybind11 init method with direct references to class
    # constructors to avoid ambiguous template compile error on gcc 14
    # Submitted upstream in https://github.com/Qiskit/qiskit-aer/pull/2416
    - 0002-Fix-pybind11-init-template-errors.patch
    # Switch from pybind11 enum_ to native_enum to avoid ambiguous template
    # compile error on gcc 14
    # Submitted upstream in https://github.com/Qiskit/qiskit-aer/pull/2417
    - 0003-Switch-from-pybind11-enum_-to-native_enum.patch

build:
  number: ${{ build }}
  string: ${{ "cpu" if not has_cuda else "cuda" + (cuda_compiler_version | replace('.', '')) }}_py${{ python | version_to_buildstring }}h${{ hash }}_${{ build if not has_cuda else build + 200 }}
  skip: has_cuda and not (linux and x86_64)
  script:
    env:
      DISABLE_DEPENDENCY_INSTALL: ON
      DISABLE_CONAN: ON
    content:
      - if: unix and has_cuda
        then:
          - |
            export CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=1
            export CUQUANTUM_ROOT="$PREFIX"
            export CUTENSOR_ROOT="$PREFIX"
            export AER_THRUST_BACKEND=CUDA
            export AER_ENABLE_CUQUANTUM=ON
            {% raw %}# cuQuantum only supports Volta+{% endraw %}
            export AER_CUDA_ARCH="7.0;7.5;8.0;8.6;9.0+PTX"

      - if: win
        then: |
          SET "CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH=%PREFIX%"
          SET "CMAKE_ARGS=%CMAKE_ARGS% -DUSE_BUNDLED_BLAS_WIN:BOOL=FALSE"
          SET "CMAKE_GENERATOR=Ninja"
        else: export CMAKE_GENERATOR="Ninja"

      - ${{ PYTHON }} -m pip install . -vv

requirements:
  build:
    - if: build_platform != target_platform
      then:
        - python
        - cross-python_${{ target_platform }}
        - pybind11 >=3.0
    - ${{ compiler('cxx') }}
    - ${{ stdlib("c") }}
    - if: has_cuda
      then: ${{ compiler('cuda') }}
    - cmake !=3.17,>=3.8
    - if: linux
      then: libgomp
    - if: osx
      then: llvm-openmp
    - if: win
      then: vcomp14
    - ninja
  host:
    - libblas
    - liblapack
    # Upstream builds with 3.1.2 but that has no osx arm64 build. Instead we
    # pin the latest known working version. See
    # https://github.com/Qiskit/qiskit-aer/issues/1742 where problems were
    # reported with 3.10.3
    - nlohmann_json <=3.10.2
    # In early 2025, mkl started getting installed randomly on Linux about 50%
    # of the time. When this happens the build seems to link to it but not add
    # it to the run exports. Maybe mkl builds could work but we have been doing
    # fine without them so just make sure they do not happen for now.
    - nomkl
    - pip
    - python
    - pybind11 >=3.0
    - scikit-build >=0.11.0
    - setuptools >=40.1.0
    - spdlog
    - if: has_cuda
      then:
        - cuda-version ${{ cuda_compiler_version }}.*
        # Qiskit Aer now uses both
        - custatevec
        - cutensornet
  run:
    - python
    - numpy >=1.16.3
    - psutil >=5
    - python-dateutil >=2.8.0
    - qiskit >=0.45.2
    - scipy >=1.0
    - if: has_cuda
      then: ${{ pin_compatible('cuda-version', upper_bound='x', lower_bound='x') }}
  ignore_run_exports:
    from_package:
      - if: has_cuda
        then: cuda-version
      # Only headers used from spdlog
      - spdlog

tests:
  # NOTE: as of https://github.com/conda-forge/qiskit-aer-feedstock/pull/62
  # we can no longer test import of the CUDA build even this way because
  # custatevec now pulls in a binary not provided by conda-forge.
  # - export CUDA_STUB="$PREFIX/lib/stubs/libcuda.so"
  # - LD_PRELOAD="$CUDA_STUB" python -c "import qiskit_aer"
  # - python -c "import qiskit_aer"
  - python:
      imports:
        - if: build_platform == target_platform and not has_cuda
          then: qiskit_aer
      pip_check: true
  - files:
      source:
        - .stestr.conf
        - test/*
    requirements:
      run:
        - ddt
        - ipython
        - matplotlib-base
        - numpy
        - pip
        - seaborn
        - scipy
        - stestr
        - sympy
        - if: linux and has_cuda
          then: cuda-driver-dev
    script:
      # Ignore tests that require qiskit_qasm3_import which is not packaged in
      # conda-forge by using `-E test_save_statevector_for_qasm3_circuit`
      #
      # Ignore shot_branching tests that seem to be flaky in the upstream tests as well.
      #
      # Ignore test_switch_register_with_classical_expression because Qiskit 2.1
      # broke these tests. It appears qiskit-aer code was wrong (passing list of
      # CLbit to Var() when only passing a single Clbit is documented as
      # supported). See
      # https://github.com/Qiskit/qiskit-aer/pull/2361
      #
      # Ignore test_mps_svd_method because it sometimes fails with `Wrong SVD calculations: A != USV*`
      # on Windows. No idea why.
      - if: build_platform == target_platform and not has_cuda
        then: stestr run -E "test_save_statevector_for_qasm3_circuit|TestShotBranching|test_dynamic_circuit_with_shot_branching|test_switch_register_with_classical_expression|test_mps_svd_method"

      # The following used to work for CUDA builds but broke with 0.15.0. See
      # https://github.com/Qiskit/qiskit-aer/issues/2213
      #
      # Ignore _GPU tests for CUDA builds because the environment does not have a GPU
      # - export CUDA_STUB="$PREFIX/lib/stubs/libcuda.so"
      # - LD_PRELOAD="$CUDA_STUB" stestr run -E 'test_save_statevector_for_qasm3_circuit|_GPU'

about:
  summary: Qiskit Aer - High performance simulators for Qiskit
  license: Apache-2.0
  license_file: LICENSE.txt
  homepage: https://github.com/Qiskit/qiskit-aer

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - leofang
    - wshanks
    - basnijholt

about.summary: Qiskit Aer - High performance simulators for Qiskit
